perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "Control" or "Controlled" shall mean possession of more than fifty percent (50%) of the share capital of a corporation or other business entity, and/or the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a corporation or other entity whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "Control" or "Controlled" shall mean possession of more than twenty-five percent (25%) of the share capital of a corporation or other business entity, and/or the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a corporation or other entity whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the definition of 'Control'. Originally, 'Control' required more than 50% ownership, but now, it's redefined as more than 25%. This could contradict Section 1.6 'Change of Control' which relies on the original meaning of 'Control'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 "Change of Control" shall mean (i) any change, sale, merger, reorganization, or any other event or action that results in a third party, which is a material competitor to the other Party to this agreement, acquiring: (a) all or substantially all of the business or assets of a Party relating to this Agreement, (b) Control, directly or indirectly, of such Party (and/or any corporate entity that Controls, directly or indirectly, such Party), or (ii) any assignment or delegation of, sale or transfer of a Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement (or any part hereof) to a third party. Notwithstanding anything in the immediately preceding paragraph to the contrary, where the Party in question is Dexcel, any of the foregoing events or actions shall not be considered a Change of Control where any one or more of the relevant third party or parties referred to in clause (i) above is (A) a Family Member, or (B) any entity Controlled by Mr. **** and/or a Family Member.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Control' from requiring more than 50% ownership to more than 25%. This directly contradicts section 1.6 'Change of Control' because a 'Change of Control' event is triggered when a third party acquires 'Control' of a Party. With the reduced threshold of 25%, a 'Change of Control' event could be triggered much more easily, contradicting the original intent and potentially altering the obligations and consequences outlined in section 1.6. The original definition of 'Control' is critical to the interpretation of 'Change of Control' in Section 1.6, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.31 "Product" shall mean tablets containing the APIs Celecoxib/Amlodipine in three dosage strengths (200/10mg, 200/5mg and 200/2.5mg), Labelled with the Livery and in Packs.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.31 "Product" shall mean tablets containing the APIs Celecoxib/Amlodipine in three dosage strengths (200/10mg, 200/5mg and 200/2.5mg) or any other strength agreed to between parties, Labelled with the Livery and in Packs.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Product' is being modified to now includes tablets of the APIs Celecoxib/Amlodipine in any strength, agreed by both parties. This introduces an ambiguity because Section 3.4 specifies the Supply Prices for only the three original dosage strengths, creating an in-text contradiction about the price of the new 'Products' now under the defintion in 1.31.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 The Supply Prices for the Product shall be: Strength Pack Size Supply Price/Pack (in US Dollars) 200/10mg Bottle **** tablets **** 200/10mg Bottle **** tablets **** 200/5mg Bottle **** tablets **** 200/5mg Bottle **** tablets **** 200/2.5mg Bottle **** tablets **** 200/2.5mg Bottle **** tablets ****
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 1.31 expands the definition of 'Product' to include any strength agreed upon by both parties. This directly contradicts Section 3.4, which specifies supply prices only for the original three dosage strengths (200/10mg, 200/5mg, and 200/2.5mg). The contradiction arises because the modified definition introduces 'Products' with unspecified strengths and no associated pricing in Section 3.4, creating ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the price of these new 'Products'. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Supply Commencement Date of the Product ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least **** of the Initial Term or a Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Effective Date ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least **** of the Initial Term or a Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Initial Term commences from the Supply Commencement Date of the product. The modified text, however, states that the Initial Term commences from the Effective Date. This creates an in-text contradiction since the original date to calculate the Initial Term is being swapped to the Effective Date, in case the Supply Commencement Date happens to be later then the Effective date. If Section 3.6.3 is reached and Kitov fails to make any payment due hereunder within ninety (90) days following the original due date, then it will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement, but in case the change is made, that might not be the case, this change creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6.3 With the exception of amounts in legitimate dispute, in the event that Kitov fails to make any payment due hereunder within ninety (90) days following the original due date, it shall be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and shall entitle Dexcel, in its sole discretion, to terminate this Agreement with immediate effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the Initial Term starts from the Supply Commencement Date, while the modified text states it starts from the Effective Date. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that a payment failure within 90 days of the original due date is a material breach. If the Supply Commencement Date is significantly later than the Effective Date, then using the Effective Date to calculate the Initial Term could lead to a payment failure that is *not* considered a material breach, as the 90-day period might not have elapsed when calculated from the Effective Date. This directly impacts and weakens the material breach clause, making its enforcement uncertain and creates a logical inconsistency, as it is now unclear when the payment needs to be made by to be considered a material breach. This falls under the definition of ambiguities as it creates an in-text contradiction.
